reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N–O bond rearrangement in nitroacetate during nitrile oxide formation

reagents: Phenylacetylene (1.0 equiv, 0.5 M in TBAB/EG 1:5 DES)
feed_streams.reasoning: Dipolarophile dissolved in DES; kept separate from nitroacetate until mixing point to avoid premature nitrile oxide dimerization at room temperature during storage

reagents: Ethyl nitroacetate (2.0 equiv, 1.0 M in TBAB/EG 1:5 DES)
feed_streams.reasoning: Nitrile oxide precursor in DES; separate stream allows precise stoichiometric control via flow rate ratio

reagents.name: Phenylacetylene
reagents.notes: Terminal alkyne; reacts regioselectively with nitrile oxide to give 3,5-disubstituted isoxazole

reagents.name: Ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates ethoxycarbonyl nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C; 2 equiv used to ensure full conversion of alkyne

reagents.name: Tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Forms deep eutectic solvent with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium promoting nitrile oxide formation

reagents.name: Ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Hydrogen bond donor in DES; high boiling point compatible with 120°C operation

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration/tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ, facilitated by the DES medium at 120°C
Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide 1,3-dipole with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate regioselectively

stream_logic.reasoning: Dipolarophile in DES; stable at room temperature; separate pump allows stoichiometric control
Nitrile oxide precursor in DES; kept separate to prevent premature reaction or nitrile oxide dimerization before reaching heated zone

mixing_order_reasoning: Streams A and B should be mixed at a T-mixer immediately before entering the heated coil reactor. Rapid mixing followed by immediate heating ensures the nitrile oxide generated in situ is trapped by phenylacetylene before it can dimerize to furoxan. The DES is viscous (~50-100 cP at RT) so preheating feed lines to ~60°C may improve pumpability and mixing quality.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; the reaction proceeds under air in batch protocol
quench_reasoning: Reaction reaches full conversion in 15 min; product is stable. Offline aqueous workup and extraction with DCM suffice for purification. No inline quench needed.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photoinitiation involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: well-established nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate and [3+2] cycloaddition. Moderate uncertainty about DES pumpability in flow — TBAB/EG is viscous at RT and may require heated feed lines or dilution. The 120°C temperature in a pressurized flow system (back-pressure regulator needed since EG bp = 197°C but to ensure no gas bubble issues from trace water loss) should be straightforward. The 2.0 equiv of nitroacetate is used to compensate for competing nitrile oxide dimerization — in flow with better mixing, it may be possible to reduce to 1.5 equiv. Residence time of ~15 min in a coil reactor at 120°C is very practical for flow.
